creator: osa@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Thu, Jun 20, 2013 at 02:01:48PM -0400, Steve Wills wrote:
 > > Any chance to use stock /bin/sh?
 
 There is one more question from my side: is there no /bin/sh on linux?
 
 > There are several problems with that idea. First you'd have to make sure
 > that all the calls are compatible and second you'd have to make it read
 > .bashrc. See the docs:
 > 
 > http://www.modrails.com/documentation/Users%20guide%20Apache.html#about_environment_variables
 
 The quote from the end of chapter.
 
 Because this chapter is meant for beginners, it assumes that the reader
 uses the bash shell. This chapter does not describe instructions for zsh,
 csh or other shells. We assume that users of other shells are familiar with
 the Bourne shell syntax, and know how to apply the instructions in this
 chapter in their shells' native syntaxes.
 
 > Sure, we could tell people to substitute .bashrc for .profile in a
 > pkg-message, but then we've really gone beyond "porting" (making the
 > application work on our platform) and into really making the application
 > fit our platform, IMHO.
 
 OK from me, see above.
  
 > So to me it's all rather not worth it to save a few people the trouble of
 > installing the bash package, which for me is less than 1 megabyte to
 > download the binary package and took less than 1 minute to compile
 > according to my logs.
 
 Probably, cheapest way but isn't right.
 
 -- 
 Sergey A. Osokin
 osa@FreeBSD.org
